text: This phone mount was fairly easy to install. I took it on a ride to see how things would shake out, expecting a couple small issues, but by the end I was counting the minutes until I was home. First, the side arms that secure your phone are right on the power and volume buttons, so it kept trying to shut my phone down. Very frustrating when you want to take a quick look at a map before the road splits and have to back out of a shutdown menu, and are also trying to avoid the "emergency call" button. So I opened up the little kickstand thing to see if that would help elevate the phone enough to keep the side arms from touching the buttons, and the elastic band kept squeezing the unlock button for the side arms. Finally ended up using the top two elastic bands, side arms, and kickstand to keep my phone in place and not shutting down my phone. No matter how much I tightened the washer thing around the ball joint my phone would not stay upright. It tipped backward over the handlebars. Just to see if it was my phone being not centered, I folded the kickstand up and used the elastic and side arms like I had for the start of my ride. Still tipped over. I turned the whole phone holding assembly upside down both ways and it still fell over. I put it sideways and it twisted and fell. The part that attached to the handlebars didn't move, it was the ball joint that kept slipping. And then the noise. That ball joint was squeaking so loud from the vibrations of my bike on anything other than a perfectly paved road that I seriously considered stopping, removing it, and hurling it into the nearest cornfield. The only reason I didn't was because I didn't want to waste any more time dealing with this thing on my ride. Only reason I didn't try to return it was because it was used outside.

text: When I purchased the Vanmass car phone mount, "military grade durability" was high among my priorities. Everything about the mount is first rate. The adhesive used with the suction base, adjustable/extending arm, swivel ball mount, a secure and protective grip; this is the best mount I've ever had, and I've never had my iPhone in a better position relative to the driver's seat. The only downside was that the four threaded tabs inside the ring that clamps to the ball broke off. This occurred past the return date, but I assumed that Vanmass would like to know about a weak link in its otherwise strong chain, so I submitted a note on their Support line.I was a bit surprised and greatly pleased to be contacted by a member of the Vanmass Support Team, who set things in motion to mail a replacement mount to me. Clearly, she was genuinely interested in understanding details about the defect rather than just hurrying to get this service ticket closed. I provided photos and a full description. In a follow-up email, I learned that the company had conducted a thorough investigation and discovered that a small batch of defective products entered the market due to a supplier that supplied non-compliant materials. Vanmass severed their relationship with that supplier and worked with Amazon to remove and destroy any remaining faulty inventory. The replacement unit they sent me was made with improved materials. I've been using it for three weeks now, and it has perrformed flawlessly.I was grateful for the attention given to my note. Not only did someone respond quickly, but I was shown the utmost courtesy, relevant information, and a replacement. Outstanding after-the-sale service. Happily, the new mount arrived the night before I left on a road trip from Tennessee to Michigan, so I was able to install early on the morning I left and enjoyed using it the entire trip. In my view, Vanmass' excellent product is matched by its world-class service.

text: Very good cell phone holder made by a very good company! If you are looking for a solid holder that can handle large phones, then look no further. I have a Samsung S23Ultra in an Otterbox case with ThinOptics reading glasses case attached and this holder holds the whole package securely (sometimes too securely). Clamping and releasing the phone is quick and easy with very little vibration driving down the road. Suction cup mount to the windshield is best. The vent mount is less secure and needs to be reset periodically. Not hard to do, but more messing around than the suction cup which is "set & forget"Why did I say "sometimes too securely"? Because it clamps the phone so tightly that its grip is stronger than the plastic ball mount. As my son found out, removing the phone enough times without pushing the release button will result in the fingers of the ball mount breaking.I contacted Vanmass to see if I could buy a replacement head and they let me know that the unit was still under warranty and promptly sent me a whole new unit. Great to see when a company stands behind their product!Summary: Solid mount and excellent customer service. I highly recommend (just remember to push the release button!)
